content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

All four amendments identify real, verified issues. The u32 truncation (fr-16-554c5cb5) is a straightforward defensive fix. The serde(other) contract change (fr-16-7dda326b) is a real behavioral shift that no consumer currently handles. The stale-round reconciliation bug (fr-16-276bec97) is confirmed: max_round is derived from existing mapping rows, so when a later round resolves PE findings (writing zero rows), the earlier round's stale mappings persist. The mapped_to_bead_id validation gap (fr-16-5920df5b) is confirmed across all three files: no whitelist check exists, only an existence check via br show.

## Votes

- `fr-16-554c5cb5`: Accept
  Verified at service.rs:2491-2494. The read path uses `.as_u64().map(|v| v as u32)` — an unchecked truncating cast. The write path at line 2380 correctly uses `Number::from(u32)`, so values written in normal operation are always in u32 range. However, corrupted or manually-edited journal data could contain values above u32::MAX, and the current cast would silently produce wrong completion_round values rather than treating them as missing. Switching to `.and_then(|v| u32::try_from(v).ok())` is a minimal, zero-risk defensive fix that converts out-of-range values to None.
- `fr-16-7dda326b`: Accept
  Verified at model.rs:284-302. The `#[serde(other)]` attribute on `Unknown` fundamentally changes the deserialization contract: previously, an unrecognized event type string in a non-trailing journal line would fail to parse and trigger AppError::CorruptRecord in read_journal_from_path (fs.rs:3095-3102); now it silently deserializes as Unknown. The comment at lines 297-299 says 'consumers should skip Unknown events,' but no consumer actively filters them, and read_journal_from_path returns all events unfiltered. This is a real contract change that needs either (a) filtering Unknown events in read_journal_from_path before returning, or (b) documented verification that all 27+ consumers handle Unknown gracefully. The amendment correctly flags both concerns.
- `fr-16-276bec97`: Accept
  Verified at success_reconciliation.rs:729-730. max_round is computed as `filtered.iter().filter_map(|m| m.completion_round).max()` — derived solely from existing mapping rows. When round N recorded PE mappings but round N+1's final-review aggregate resolved or rejected all PE findings (writing zero mapping rows), max_round stays at N and `retain` keeps the stale round-N mappings. The reconstruction logic at lines 942-948 only adds missing mappings from the latest aggregate — it never removes existing ones. So the stale round-N mappings persist in `unverified` and get verified/commented as if they were still authoritative. The fix must derive the authoritative round from the latest final-review aggregate or run snapshot, and discard earlier-round mappings when the latest aggregate contains no PE amendments.
- `fr-16-5920df5b`: Accept
  Verified across all three files. Engine (lines 7616-7622) only checks for None/empty strings. Reconciliation planned_elsewhere.rs (lines 70-96) only verifies the bead exists via `br show`. Final review merge (lines 188-226) normalizes and resolves reviewer conflicts but never validates against the allowed set. The PlannedElsewherePromptContext whitelist exists in project_run_record/service.rs but is never passed to or checked by execute_final_review_panel or validate_vote_payload. Template guidance at template_catalog.rs:234-242 instructs reviewers to use planned-elsewhere IDs, but this is prompt-level guidance with no code enforcement. Since mapped_to_bead_id suppresses restart_required (line 1573: `has_non_planned_elsewhere` checks if ANY amendment lacks a mapping), a hallucinated but existing bead ID is a confirmed safety bypass of the fix loop.
